summary: PR #813 introduced an API v1 distributed relay bridge that routed raw OpenAI-style messages through relay-owned state, violating the relay-blind E2EE invariant until Prompt 1 containment, Prompt 2 E2EE restoration, and Prompt 3 safeguards landed.
impact: The regression created confirmed code-level plaintext exposure risk in distributed API v1 relay state; containment temporarily fail-closed distributed API v1 relay behavior to a safe error path, and no confirmed exploitation is recorded in repository evidence.
fix: Prompt 1 fail-closed unsafe distributed API v1 relay dispatch (PR #834), Prompt 2 restored distributed relay operation through relay-blind E2EE envelope handling (PR #836), and Prompt 3 added static/runtime/network/log/test/docs anti-regression safeguards (PR #842).
lessons: Authentication hardening alone (e.g., PR #831 token gating) is not a substitute for relay blindness; relay-owned state must remain ciphertext-only, and that invariant needs explicit automated enforcement.
